name: coding_agent
description: A coding-agent memory scenario: engineering facts about a codebase/ops, and natural-language queries a coding agent would issue, each labelled with the memories that should surface. Designed so token overlap gives the default hashing embedder signal; swap in a real embedder for higher-fidelity scores.

memories.label: deploy_ship
memories.text: Deployment to production runs through `make ship`: it builds, tags a release, and pushes the image. The staging smoke-test step is flaky and frequently needs one retry.

memories.label: deploy_rollback
memories.text: To roll back a bad release, run `make rollback VERSION=<prev>`; it re-points the load balancer to the previous image without a full redeploy.

memories.label: db_migrations
memories.text: Database migrations live in db/migrations and are applied with `make migrate`. Never edit an already-applied migration — add a new one.

memories.label: db_conn_pool
memories.text: The Postgres connection pool max is 20; exceeding it causes 'too many clients' errors under load. Batch queries instead of opening new connections.

memories.label: auth_jwt
memories.text: Authentication uses short-lived JWTs signed with RS256; the public keys are served from /.well-known/jwks.json and rotated weekly.

memories.label: auth_rate_limit
memories.text: The login endpoint is rate-limited to 5 attempts per minute per IP; repeated failures return HTTP 429 with a Retry-After header.

memories.label: testing_cmd
memories.text: Run the unit tests with `make test` and the integration suite with `make integration`; `make check` runs both.

memories.label: testing_flaky
memories.text: The test_websocket_reconnect integration test is flaky due to a timing race; if it fails once, re-run it before investigating.

memories.label: style_naming
memories.text: Code style: snake_case for functions and variables, PascalCase for types. Run `make fmt` before committing or CI rejects the diff.

memories.label: style_errors
memories.text: Prefer returning explicit error values over raising exceptions in the storage layer; exceptions are reserved for programmer bugs.

memories.label: perf_cache
memories.text: The product catalog is cached in Redis with a 5-minute TTL. After a price change, invalidate the catalog:* keys or users see stale prices.

memories.label: perf_n_plus_1
memories.text: The orders list endpoint had an N+1 query bug, fixed by eager-loading line items with a single join. Watch for this pattern in new list endpoints.

memories.label: arch_services
memories.text: The system is a modular monolith: web, billing, and notifications are separate packages that deploy as one binary. Cross-package calls go through interfaces, not direct imports.

memories.label: arch_events
memories.text: Domain events are published to an in-process event bus; notifications subscribes to OrderPlaced. Do not add synchronous cross-service HTTP calls.

memories.label: secrets_mgmt
memories.text: Secrets are injected as environment variables from Vault at boot; never commit secrets or read them from files. Local dev uses a gitignored .env.local.

memories.label: logging_conv
memories.text: Structured logging only: use the log helper with key=value fields, never printf. Levels: error for actionable failures, info for lifecycle, debug behind a flag.

memories.label: incident_oom
memories.text: Incident: a memory leak in the image-resize worker OOM-killed pods hourly; root cause was an unbounded LRU cache. Fixed by capping the cache at 512 entries.

memories.label: onboarding_setup
memories.text: New developer setup: clone, run `make bootstrap` to install deps and git hooks, copy .env.example to .env.local, then `make dev` to start the stack.

memories.label: api_versioning
memories.text: The public API is versioned by URL prefix (/v1, /v2). Never break a shipped version — add a new one and deprecate the old with a sunset header.

memories.label: git_workflow
memories.text: Branch from main, open a pull request, squash-merge. CI must be green and one review is required before merge; direct pushes to main are blocked.

memories.label: mobile_build
memories.text: The iOS build requires Xcode 16 and runs via `fastlane beta`; signing certificates live in the shared 1Password vault, not the repo.

memories.label: search_infra
memories.text: Full-text search is backed by OpenSearch; the indexer runs as a nightly cron plus a real-time tailer. A full reindex is `make reindex` and takes about 20 minutes.

queries.text: how do I deploy to production?
what is the command to roll back a release to the previous version?
how are database migrations applied and where do they live?
we are getting too many clients errors from postgres under load
how does authentication and JWT signing work?
why am I getting HTTP 429 on the login endpoint?
how do I run the unit and integration tests?
the websocket reconnect integration test keeps failing
what naming conventions and code formatting should I follow?
how is the product catalog cached and how do I invalidate stale prices?
how are secrets managed and injected at boot?
what is the git branching and pull request merge process?
how does the in-process domain event bus work?
how do I set up my development environment as a new engineer?
what security practices does the project follow?
